steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage presents two facts: Quito residents use parks more (82.9%) than Lima residents (72.7%), yet Quito residents reported much lower access to desired amenities near parks than Lima residents did. The main idea is the conclusion the author draws from this contrast.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Even though the study found that parks in Lima are more likely to be close to other amenities than parks in Quito are, Quito has more amenities overall than Lima does.: The passage only discusses amenities near parks, not overall city amenities. This introduces a comparison the passage never makes. ✗
  • Choice B — The study's findings suggest that an increase in the number of amenities near city parks would likely increase park use in Quito but not in Lima.: The passage draws no conclusion about what would increase park use; it only says the current difference can't be explained by amenity access. ✗
  • Choice C — The study's finding that a greater proportion of residents use parks in Quito than in Lima is partly due to the greater prevalence of parks in Quito.: The passage says nothing about the number or prevalence of parks in either city. ✗
  • Choice D — Although the study found that a greater proportion of residents use parks in Quito than in Lima, that difference isn't due to greater access to amenities near parks in Quito.: This directly restates the passage's own conclusion: higher Quito usage despite lower amenity access means amenity access doesn't explain the gap. ✓
D is correct.
